heading: Temporary extension of section 794
label: 798A Temporary extension of section 794
text: The provisions of , as amended and extended by section 1(a)(29) of the Emergency Powers Continuation Act (), as further amended by Public Law 12, Eighty-third Congress, in addition to coming into full force and effect in time of war shall remain in full force and effect until six months after the termination of the national emergency proclaimed by the President on (Proc. 2912, 3 C.F.R., 1950 Supp., p. 71), or such earlier date as may be prescribed by concurrent resolution of the Congress, and acts which would give rise to legal consequences and penalties under section 794 when performed during a state of war shall give rise to the same legal consequences and penalties when they are performed during the period above provided for.section 794 of this title66 Stat. 333December 16, 1950
notes: References in TextSection 1(a)(29) of the Emergency Powers Continuation Act () as further amended by Public Law 12, Eighty-third Congress, referred to in text, was formerly set out as a note under and was repealed by section 7 of act .66 Stat. 333section 791 of this titleJune 30, 1953Proc. 2912, 3 C.F.R., 1950 Supp., p. 71, referred to in text, probably should refer to Proc. 2914, which is set out as a note preceding , War and National Defense.section 1 of Title 50
Amendments1990— renumbered the second as this section.Pub. L. 101–647section 798 of this title
Termination of National EmergencyDeclaration of national emergency in effect on , was terminated two years from that date by , War and National Defense.Sept. 14, 1976section 1601 of Title 50
